FOOTER.BRAND_DESCRIPTION: Infrastructure as Data, Bicep as Code. Secure workspace for Azure platform teams.
FOOTER.LEGAL: Built for cloud governance and delivery teams

BRANDING.DESCRIPTION: Simplify Azure infrastructure configuration with visual tools, automated Bicep generation, and seamless DevOps integration.

FEATURES.DESIGNER_DESC: Configure Azure resources with an intuitive UI
FEATURES.SECURITY_DESC: Microsoft Entra ID integration with RBAC

FORM.TITLE: Welcome back
FORM.SUBTITLE: Sign in to your account to continue

CROSS_CONFIG_REFS.TITLE: Cross-config references
CROSS_CONFIG_REFS.DESCRIPTION: Reference resources from other configurations in the same project to create cross-config dependencies (e.g., shared Log Analytics Workspace, common ACR).

CROSS_CONFIG_REFS.ADD_DIALOG_NO_RESOURCES: No resources available in other configurations of this project.

COMING_SOON.IDENTITY_DESC: Configure managed identities, RBAC role assignments, and access policies for this resource. Available in a future release.
COMING_SOON.CONFIG_DESC: Manage environment variables, connection strings, and secrets for App Services, Container Apps, and Azure Functions. Available in a future release.

ROLE_ASSIGNMENTS.DESCRIPTION: Configure which resources this resource can access using its managed identity.
ROLE_ASSIGNMENTS.EMPTY: No role assignments configured. Add a role assignment to grant this resource access to other resources.
ROLE_ASSIGNMENTS.REMOVE_MESSAGE: Remove the role "{{role}}" on "{{target}}"? This action cannot be undone.
ROLE_ASSIGNMENTS.REMOVE_ERROR: Unable to remove the role assignment. Please try again.
ROLE_ASSIGNMENTS.UPDATE_IDENTITY_ERROR: Unable to update the managed identity. Please try again.
ROLE_ASSIGNMENTS.SWITCH_UAI_TOOLTIP: Only one User Assigned Identity can be assigned — switch to a different one

GRANTED_RIGHTS.DESCRIPTION: Role assignments granted through this User-Assigned Managed Identity. Each entry shows which source resource accesses which target resource, and with which role.
GRANTED_RIGHTS.EMPTY: No resource is using this identity yet.
GRANTED_RIGHTS.LOAD_ERROR: Unable to load granted rights. Please try again.

USED_BY.DESCRIPTION: Resources that use this managed identity. You can unlink a resource to delete the role assignments that go through it.
USED_BY.EMPTY: No resource is using this identity yet.
USED_BY.UNLINK_RESOURCE_MESSAGE: Delete all role assignments of "{{resource}}" going through this identity? Other rights of this identity are not affected.
USED_BY.UNLINK_ERROR: Unable to unlink the resource. Please try again.

APP_SETTINGS.DESCRIPTION: Configure environment variables for this compute resource. Reference outputs from other resources or set static values.
APP_SETTINGS.EMPTY: No app settings configured. Add an app setting to inject environment variables or resource outputs.
APP_SETTINGS.SECTION_SECRETS_DESC: Sensitive values stored securely in Azure Key Vault.
APP_SETTINGS.SECTION_VG_DESC: Values injected from Azure DevOps variable groups at deploy time.
APP_SETTINGS.SECTION_OUTPUTS_DESC: Values generated dynamically by other resources.
APP_SETTINGS.SECTION_STATIC_DESC: Plain text values set per environment — avoid storing secrets here.
APP_SETTINGS.REMOVE_MESSAGE: Remove the app setting "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
APP_SETTINGS.REMOVE_ERROR: Unable to remove the app setting. Please try again.

ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SUBTITLE_STATIC: Set a variable name and a value for each environment.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SUBTITLE_SELECT_SOURCE: Choose the resource whose output you want to export as an environment variable.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SUBTITLE_SELECT_OUTPUT: Choose which output value to inject into this resource.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SUBTITLE_NAME: Choose the environment variable name. A suggestion based on the source resource and output is pre-filled.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.NO_SOURCES: No other resources found in this configuration.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.NO_OUTPUTS: No outputs available for this resource type.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.KV_SUBTITLE_SELECT: Choose the Key Vault that holds the secret you want to reference.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.KV_NO_VAULTS: No Key Vault resources found in this configuration.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.KV_SUBTITLE_SECRET: Enter the name of the secret stored in the Key Vault.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.KV_NO_ACCESS_DESC: This resource does not have the "{{role}}" role on the selected Key Vault. Without it, the resource cannot read the secret at runtime. You can add the role assignment in the Role Assignments section.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.KV_ACCESS_OK: This resource has the required access to read secrets from this Key Vault.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.KV_NO_ACCESS_REMINDER: Remember to add the Key Vault Secrets User role assignment.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.ERROR: Unable to add the app setting. Please try again.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SENSITIVE_CHOICE_SUBTITLE: This output contains sensitive data (keys, passwords, connection strings). Choose how to handle it.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SENSITIVE_EXPORT_KV_DESC: Recommended — The value will be stored as a secret in a Key Vault and referenced securely.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SENSITIVE_DIRECT_DESC: Not recommended — The value will be visible in plain text in generated Bicep.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SENSITIVE_DIRECT_WARNING: ⚠️ This value contains sensitive data (access keys, passwords). It will be visible in plain text in generated Bicep files and deployment environment variables. Use a Key Vault reference to secure this value.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SENSITIVE_SECRET_NAME_HINT: The secret name in the Key Vault (auto-generated)
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SENSITIVE_ASSIGN_ROLE: Assign Key Vault Secrets User role
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.OPTION_VIA_BICEPPARAM_DESC: The value will be provided at deployment time. A secure parameter will be declared and initialized to '' in the bicepparam file.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.OPTION_DIRECT_IN_KV_DESC: The value will be entered manually in the Azure Key Vault portal. No Bicep parameter will be generated for this value.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.SECRET_NAME_HINT: The name under which the secret will be stored in Key Vault
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.PIPELINE_VARIABLE_NAME_HINT: Name as it will be used in $(variableName) in the pipeline
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.STATIC_STANDARD_DESC: The value is set per environment (dev, staging, prod). Ideal for non-sensitive configuration.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.STATIC_SECRET_DESC: The value is stored in a Key Vault and referenced securely via @Microsoft.KeyVault(SecretUri=...).
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.STATIC_ENV_VALUES_DESC: Set a specific value for each environment. Values will be injected via Bicep parameters.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.STATIC_VG_DESC: The value comes from an Azure DevOps pipeline variable group, injected at deploy time.
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.STATIC_STEP3B_TITLE: How is the secret value set?
ADD_APP_SETTING_DIALOG.STATIC_SECRET_VG_DESC: The value is injected from a pipeline variable group into the Key Vault secret at deploy time.

ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.SUBTITLE_SELECT: Choose the resource this resource should have access to
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.SUBTITLE_CONFIGURE: Select the identity type and role to assign
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.NO_TARGETS: No other resources found in this resource group.
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.NO_ROLES: No roles available for this resource type.
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.ERROR: Unable to assign the role. Please try again.
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.NO_IDENTITIES: No User-Assigned Identities found in this configuration.
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.CREATE_IDENTITY_ERROR: Unable to create the identity. Please try again.
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.SELECT_SOURCE_RESOURCE_DESC: Choose the resource that will use the managed identity to access other resources.
ADD_ROLE_DIALOG.SELECT_TARGET_RESOURCE_DESC: Choose the resource this managed identity will access.

BLOB_CORS.TITLE: Blob service CORS
BLOB_CORS.DESCRIPTION: Define origins, methods, headers, and cache duration applied to the blob service.
BLOB_CORS.EMPTY: No CORS rule configured for the blob service.

TABLE_CORS.TITLE: Table service CORS
TABLE_CORS.DESCRIPTION: Define origins, methods, headers, and cache duration applied to the table service.
TABLE_CORS.EMPTY: No CORS rule configured for the table service.

CORS_COMMON.ORIGINS_HINT: Use * or an exact http/https origin. Wildcard subdomains such as https://*.contoso.com are supported.
CORS_COMMON.METHODS_HINT: Select only the HTTP verbs your client really needs. The most restrictive rule set is the safest.
CORS_COMMON.ALLOWED_HEADERS_HINT: Add only the request headers your client actually sends. Prefixes ending with * help reduce deployment errors.
CORS_COMMON.EXPOSED_HEADERS_HINT: Expose only the response headers the browser must read. Prefer focused values or an x-ms-* prefix.
CORS_COMMON.MAX_AGE_HINT: Browser preflight cache duration. 3600 seconds is a practical starting point.
CORS_COMMON.FIX_VALIDATION_ERRORS: Fix the CORS errors before saving.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_INVALID_ORIGIN: Enter * or a valid http/https origin without path or query string.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_ORIGIN_REQUIRED: Add at least one allowed origin.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_METHOD_REQUIRED: Select at least one allowed method.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_INVALID_HEADER: Enter a valid HTTP header name. The * wildcard is only allowed as a suffix.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_MAX_AGE: Max age must be a whole number greater than or equal to 0.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_DUPLICATE_VALUE: This value is already present in the rule.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_TOO_MANY_PREFIX_HEADERS: You cannot define more than 2 prefixed headers ending with *.
CORS_COMMON.ERROR_TOO_MANY_LITERAL_HEADERS: You cannot define more than 64 literal headers per list.

STORAGE_SERVICES.REMOVE_CONFIRM_MESSAGE: Are you sure you want to remove “{{name}}”?
STORAGE_SERVICES.ACTION_ERROR: An error occurred. Please try again.

LIFECYCLE_RULES.TITLE: Blob lifecycle management
LIFECYCLE_RULES.DESCRIPTION: Automatically delete blobs after a retention period. Each rule targets one or more containers.
LIFECYCLE_RULES.RULE_NAME_HINT: A unique identifier for this lifecycle rule (e.g. cleanup-logs).
LIFECYCLE_RULES.CONTAINER_NAMES_HINT: Select the blob containers this rule applies to.
LIFECYCLE_RULES.NO_CONTAINERS_HINT: No blob containers available. Add containers above first.
LIFECYCLE_RULES.TTL_HINT: Blobs will be deleted after this many days since last modification (1–36 500).
